title: Renting Boats in the Aland Islands: A Practical Guide for 2026
html:
Charter your own vessel in the Aland Islands, a Baltic archipelago where you can rent a motorboat from ports like Mariehamn and explore scores of islets. Prices for a day afloat start from around €240 per boat in 2026, and BoatHire24 is a good place to start looking for a suitable craft. You can find a skippered charter if you prefer not to self-steer.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in the Aland Islands?

Prices for a bareboat motorboat start around €240 per day in the Aland Islands, rising to over €1,000 for a crewed yacht. The cost depends on the boat type, engine power, and season. For a half-day trip on a small motorboat, expect to pay roughly €180 to €250, while a full-day charter of a cabin cruiser might run to €400. Crewed yachts and catamarans cost from €750 to €1,200 for a full day. Using BoatHire24, you can compare verified listings and see exact prices for each boat.

  • Shared tour per person: from €60
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 guests): €180
  • Catamaran full-day (8 guests): from €800
  • Crewed yacht full-day (6 guests): €1,000

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in the Aland Islands?

Yes, for any motorboat over 10 hp you need a powerboat licence recognised in Finland, and the operator will confirm the exact requirements. The Aland Islands follow Finnish maritime rules. For smaller outboards under 10 hp, often no licence is needed. Always check with the rental provider to keep within the law.

In the high season of June to August, the south-westerly wind can be steady, so plan your return by late afternoon to avoid a bumpy crossing.

Where to set off in the Aland Islands

Most charters start from the archipelago's main marinas. The principal base is Mariehamn, the capital, with modern pontoons and good facilities. Other popular departure points include the marina at Kastelholm near the medieval castle, the sheltered harbour of Käringsund, and the guest harbour at Långnäs on the main island. For a more remote start, try the deep-water jetty at Kökar or the ferry-connected port at Västra Hamnen in Mariehamn. Each offers easy access to the maze of surrounding islets.

Which boat to rent

  1. QuickSilver Activ 605 Open, a 20-foot speedboat ideal for a day trip around the inner archipelago, seating four, from €290 per day. On BoatHire24, this speedboat is listed with a 150 hp engine.
  2. Axopar 24, a modern, open-day cruiser for five, perfect for covering more ground, from €450 per half-day.
  3. Small motorboat (4-5 m), a simple outboard for exploring sheltered coves, suitable for two to three people, from €180 per half-day.
  4. Family cabin cruiser (8 m), a comfortable boat with a small cabin for six, good for a full-day or overnight, from €400 per day.
  5. Sailboat or catamaran, for those who prefer a peaceful cruise under sail, capacity up to eight, from €600 per day.

Costs by boat type, an overview

Rates here are typical for the 2026 season and can vary by month. A shared sightseeing tour, such as a two-hour trip from Mariehamn, costs from €60 per person. A full-day catamaran charter for eight guests might be from €800. Crewed luxury yachts with a skipper, for six guests, start at €1,000 per day. Prices usually include basic insurance and VAT.

At a glance

  • Price band: €60, €1,200 per person or boat
  • Peak months: June, July and August
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full-day
  • Capacity: 2, 10 guests per boat
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in the Aland Islands

Can I charter a boat for a week in the Aland Islands?

Yes, several operators offer weekly charters for motorboats and yachts, particularly from Mariehamn, at a weekly rate that is often discounted compared to daily prices.

What is the best way to explore the outer archipelagos?

The best way is to rent a cabin cruiser with overnight capability, so you can stop at the guest harbours of Kökar or Föglö without rushing back.

Are there guided tours available for non-licence holders?

Yes, you can hire a skipper for any boat rental, which removes the need for a licence and gives you local knowledge of the best anchorages.

What should I bring for a day afloat in the Aland Islands?

Pack warm clothing, sun protection, and a picnic; many islets have no shops, and the weather can change quickly even in summer.

Final thoughts

Whether you are after a fast motorboat or a quiet sailboat, the Aland Islands offer a memorable Baltic experience. To see what is available, browse the listings on BoatHire24 and find a craft that matches your needs. Boat owners can also list their own boat to earn by chartering it out.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in the Aland Islands and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
